commerce.tagline: Fictional 1U edge router with redundant routing engines
commerce.description: The Halcyon Edge 3000 is a 1U enterprise edge router with redundant routing engines, dual hot-swap power supplies, and 8 SFP+ uplink slots. It supports BGP, OSPF, and IS-IS routing at line rate, IPv4/IPv6 dual stack, and hardware-accelerated firewall + NAT. Maintenance hinges on fan tray health and routing-engine failovers. Targets WAN edge, branch aggregation, and small-DC core deployments.

common_failure_modes.name: Fan tray failure
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Replace failed fan module; verify temperature sensors return to nominal range.

common_failure_modes.name: Routing engine wedged
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Force failover to standby RE; reload primary; if it doesn't rejoin, swap RE module.

common_failure_modes.name: SFP+ port silently flapping
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Reseat transceiver, check fiber connectors; if persistent, replace transceiver and re-test with known-good port.

common_failure_modes.name: PSU fault LED
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Hot-swap the failed PSU; verify both rails are powering the chassis; log to vendor RMA queue.

pm_checklist: Confirm both routing engines are healthy + in-sync
Verify fan tray RPMs in nominal range
Inspect optical transceivers for dust + loss
Check chassis temperature sensors against baseline
Verify both PSUs supplying redundant power
Validate console port + management interface
Update firmware if needed

spare_parts.name: Routing engine module
spare_parts.description: OEM control-plane module with 16GB RAM, 240GB SSD.

consumables.name: Air filter pad
consumables.description: Replacement air filter pad for the front intake.

consumables.name: Rack-mount kit
consumables.description: Single-use rack-mount kit (rails + screws + cage nuts).

commerce.description: The Northwind FX-200 is a 48-port managed gigabit switch with PoE+ on every port (740W budget), four 10G SFP+ uplinks, and full Layer 2/3 routing. Stack-aware with up to 8 members. Targets office access, branch IDF closets, and small-campus distribution layer.

common_failure_modes.name: PoE port over-budget shutdown
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Audit PoE budget vs. connected loads; redistribute high-draw devices across stack members or upgrade PSU.

common_failure_modes.name: Stack split-brain after reboot
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Force re-elect primary; verify stack cables seated; check stacking-port LED state.

common_failure_modes.name: SFP+ uplink intermittent
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Replace transceiver with known-good unit; clean fiber endface; verify supported optic on QPL.

pm_checklist: Verify firmware on every stack member matches
Confirm PoE budget headroom is at least 20%
Inspect uplink optics for dust + loss
Check temperature sensors + fan RPM
Review error-disable port states
Validate management VLAN reachability

spare_parts.name: 740W PoE+ power supply
spare_parts.description: Hot-swap 740W AC PSU for PoE+ switching.

consumables.name: Rack-mount kit
consumables.description: Single-use rack-mount kit for the FX-200.

commerce.tagline: Fictional 2U next-gen firewall with hardware crypto offload
commerce.description: The Lumenex Q-Series is a 2U next-generation firewall with 16x SFP28 + 8x QSFP28 ports, dedicated crypto-offload ASIC for line-rate IPsec/TLS inspection, and integrated IDS/IPS. Supports active/passive HA failover. Targets enterprise perimeter, internal segmentation, and high-throughput VPN concentration.

common_failure_modes.name: Crypto ASIC overheats under sustained TLS load
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Verify chassis airflow + ambient temp; reseat ASIC card; if persistent, swap card and RMA.

common_failure_modes.name: HA pair fails to converge after failover
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Check sync interface link state; verify config sync timestamp; force re-sync from primary.

common_failure_modes.name: QSFP28 port LOS on bring-up
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Reseat transceiver; clean fiber; confirm optic on QPL; replace if persistent.

pm_checklist: Verify HA pair sync timestamp + config parity
Audit IDS/IPS signature freshness
Inspect crypto-offload ASIC temp under load
Check session table utilization vs. capacity
Validate management + HA sync interfaces
Confirm both PSUs delivering nominal voltage

spare_parts.name: Crypto-offload ASIC card
spare_parts.description: Hardware crypto-offload card for line-rate IPsec/TLS.

commerce.tagline: Fictional Wi-Fi 6E tri-radio access point for dense deployments
commerce.description: The Aristera Vector AP-7 is a tri-radio Wi-Fi 6E access point (2.4GHz, 5GHz, 6GHz) with 4x4:4 MU-MIMO on the 5/6GHz radios, integrated BLE, and a 2.5G uplink. Ceiling- or wall-mountable. Targets dense office, retail, and hospitality deployments.

common_failure_modes.name: Radio failure on 6GHz band
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Hard-reboot the AP; verify firmware; if persistent, RMA the unit (radio modules are not field-replaceable).

common_failure_modes.name: PoE link drops on extended cable runs
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Test with shorter known-good Cat6A run; verify switch port PoE+ class negotiation; check cable for kinks.

pm_checklist: Verify firmware version against fleet baseline
Confirm radio status on all three bands
Inspect mounting bracket integrity
Validate uplink negotiation rate

commerce.tagline: Fictional 16-channel DWDM mux/demux with integrated EDFA
commerce.description: The Pyralis Mesh-One is a 16-channel DWDM mux/demux on the 100GHz ITU grid with an integrated EDFA optical amplifier for single-fiber transport up to 80km without a regenerator. Passive optical chassis with one active card; suited for metro DCI, dark-fiber rentals, and small carrier transport.

common_failure_modes.name: EDFA pump-laser drift
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Check pump current vs. temp curve; recalibrate per service procedure; depot-RMA if drift exceeds threshold.

common_failure_modes.name: Channel insertion-loss out of spec
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Inspect fiber connectors at each port for contamination; clean with lint-free wipe + IPA; re-measure with OTDR.

pm_checklist: Sweep insertion loss across all 16 channels
Verify EDFA pump current + output power
Inspect connector endfaces under microscope
Validate management channel reachability
Check both DC + AC PSU rails

spare_parts.name: EDFA amplifier card
spare_parts.description: Erbium-doped fiber amplifier card with integrated pump.

consumables.name: Fiber cleaning kit
consumables.description: Single-use fiber cleaning kit (cassette + IPA wipes).

commerce.description: The Caelum CSR-100G is a QSFP28 100G LR4 single-mode optical transceiver with a 10km reach over duplex SMF. Hot-pluggable, MSA-compliant. Used as a swap-replacement part across the device-shop's optical refurb lines.

common_failure_modes.name: Tx output below -3dBm
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Replace transceiver; not field-repairable. Verify host port + fiber are not the cause first.

pm_checklist: Measure Tx + Rx optical power against datasheet
Inspect optical interface for contamination
Verify DDM/DOM telemetry on host

physical.power_requirements: Quad redundant 1500W PSUs, 100-240V AC or -48V DC

commerce.tagline: Fictional 3U L3 spine switch with 32x 400G QSFP-DD ports
commerce.description: The Vexor Atlas Workstation is a 3U Layer-3 spine switch with 32x 400G QSFP-DD ports, hardware VXLAN/EVPN, and merchant-silicon ASIC at line rate (12.8 Tbps). Quad redundant PSUs + N+1 fan trays. Targets data-center spine, AI/ML fabric, and high-radix aggregation.

common_failure_modes.name: Single fan tray fails — N+1 holds
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Hot-swap the failed fan tray; chassis remains in service throughout.

common_failure_modes.name: QSFP-DD port LOS after optic insert
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Reseat optic; verify DDM eye-diagram; replace if persistent.

common_failure_modes.name: BGP-EVPN convergence stalls on reload
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Check route-target import policies; re-establish session; capture for vendor TAC if recurring.

pm_checklist: Verify all four PSUs delivering nominal voltage
Inspect all fan trays for RPM + bearing health
Sample DDM telemetry across optic population
Audit BGP-EVPN session counters
Verify firmware against fleet baseline
Validate management interface reachability

spare_parts.name: Fan tray (N+1)
spare_parts.description: Hot-swap fan tray with redundant fan modules.

commerce.tagline: Fictional 5kVA online double-conversion UPS for rack PSU backup
commerce.description: The Zenith Tighten 5kVA is a 2U online double-conversion UPS rated for 5kVA / 4.5kW with a hot-swap battery pack and SNMP/Modbus management. Targets small-rack server backup, network-closet UPS protection, and lab-bench critical-power scenarios.

common_failure_modes.name: Battery pack capacity below 80%
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Hot-swap battery pack; recycle the depleted pack to OEM RMA channel.

common_failure_modes.name: Inverter fan stalled
common_failure_modes.typical_fix: Replace fan module; verify fan-fail alarm clears in management UI.

pm_checklist: Run battery self-test under load
Verify input voltage + frequency stability
Inspect output waveform under typical load
Audit management interface (SNMP + Modbus) reachability
Confirm fan operation

spare_parts.name: Inverter fan module
spare_parts.description: Replacement fan for the inverter compartment.
